author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Session was a SessionEnd memory extraction pass with minimal new conversation content. The startup hook loaded prior AgentOS context including open questions about RFP scoring logic and hook registration status. The session appears to have briefly touched on RFP eligibility scoring, with a prior assistant turn referencing a two-tier approach (hard gates before weighted scoring) for grant opportunity evaluation. No new code was written and no new decisions were confirmed this session.
problems: How to score RFP eligibility across multiple criteria for grant opportunities
Whether install_hooks.sh has been run to activate SessionStart/SessionEnd hooks
approaches: Two-tier scoring model: hard gates (pass/fail) first on mandatory criteria such as 245G licensure and geography, followed by weighted scoring on remaining factors
results: No new confirmed results this session — prior context loaded via SessionStart hook
Prior assistant response began outlining two-tier RFP scoring architecture consistent with earlier tentative decision

decisions.decision: Use two-tier scoring model with hard gates before weighted scoring for RFP eligibility

open_questions: Has install_hooks.sh been run to register SessionStart/SessionEnd hooks?
Are hooks now active and loading session memory correctly on SessionStart?
What weighted scoring factors apply after hard gates pass?
What threshold scores indicate high vs. low priority opportunities?
How should the scoring logic be implemented in rfp_intelligence_pro?
